title: Luscious Greek Quinoa Pastitsio
description: A yeasty, silky Greek layer dish with quinoa, shaped into nigiri-style cylinders, diabetic-friendly and bursting with creamy sauce.
instructions: Rinse quinoa, then combine with 2 cups water, bring to a boil, simmer 15 minutes until fluffy, and let cool.
In a skillet, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium, add diced onion, and sauté until translucent.
Add ground turkey to the skillet, cook until browned, and season with salt.
Whisk together low-fat Greek yogurt, egg, grated Parmesan, and cooled quinoa mixture in a bowl.
Stir the cooked turkey and onion into the quinoa mixture until well combined.
In a saucepan, whisk flour into 200ml low-fat milk, cook over medium until thickened, then stir in 1 tsp instant yeast and salt until dissolved.
Spread the quinoa mixture evenly in a 20x20 cm baking dish, then pour the béchamel over it, smoothing the surface.
Bake for 30 minutes until the top is golden and set; let rest 10 minutes before slicing into 1-cm thick cylinders resembling nigiri.
Garnish with chopped parsley (optional) before serving.
